explanation: Palmitoyl palmitamide MEA is a fatty acid amide used as an emollient and skin-conditioning agent that supports the skin's intercellular lipid layer with its fatty acid component. It is well-tolerated and used in barrier-repair and nourishing formulas for its lipid-replenishing and emollient properties.

Palmitoyl pentapeptide-4 (Pal-KTTKS) is the active peptide in Matrixyl — one of the most clinically studied signal peptides in cosmetics — that mimics a collagen breakdown fragment to stimulate fibroblasts to produce new collagen I, III, and fibronectin for improved skin firmness. It is well-tolerated, non-irritating, and among the most evidence-backed peptide actives in anti-aging skincare.

Palmitoyl pentapeptide-4 (Pal-KTTKS) is the active peptide in Matrixyl that stimulates fibroblasts to produce new collagen I, III, and fibronectin for improved skin firmness. It is well-tolerated, non-irritating, and is among the most evidence-backed peptide actives in anti-aging skincare.

Palmitoyl tetrapeptide-7 is a signal peptide that modulates interleukin-6 (IL-6) inflammatory signaling to reduce inflammation-driven skin aging and support a calmer, firmer complexion. It is well-tolerated, non-irritating, and often paired with palmitoyl tripeptide-1 in the Matrixyl 3000 combination for synergistic anti-aging activity.

Palmitoyl tripeptide-1 is a signal peptide — a component of the Matrixyl 3000 combination with palmitoyl tetrapeptide-7 — that stimulates collagen and hyaluronic acid production in fibroblasts to improve skin firmness, plumpness, and elasticity. It is well-tolerated, non-irritating, and widely used in evidence-backed anti-aging formulas.

Palmitoyl tripeptide-1 is a signal peptide and component of the Matrixyl 3000 combination that stimulates collagen and hyaluronic acid production in fibroblasts for improved skin firmness and elasticity. It is well-tolerated, non-irritating, and widely used in evidence-backed anti-aging formulas.

Palmitoyl tripeptide-37 is a palmitoylated signal peptide used in anti-aging formulas to stimulate skin renewal and support barrier integrity. It is well-tolerated, non-irritating, and used in firming and skin-renewal formulas for its extracellular matrix-stimulating properties.

Palmitoyl tripeptide-8 is a signal peptide with documented anti-inflammatory activity that reduces neurogenic skin inflammation by modulating substance P and CGRP neuropeptide pathways, calming reactive and sensitive skin. It is exceptionally well-tolerated and used in sensitive skin and neuro-calming formulas for its barrier-reinforcing and inflammation-reducing properties.

Panax ginseng (ginseng) berry extract is rich in ginsenosides and polyphenol antioxidants with documented antioxidant, anti-aging, and collagen-stimulating properties. It is well-tolerated and used in anti-aging formulas for its ability to protect skin from oxidative stress and support skin firmness.

Panax ginseng (ginseng) root extract is one of the most studied adaptogenic botanicals in skincare, providing ginsenosides, polysaccharides, and antioxidant phenolics with anti-aging, firming, and brightening properties. It is well-tolerated and widely used in K-beauty and anti-aging formulas for its collagen-stimulating and protective antioxidant content.

Panax ginseng (ginseng) seed oil is a plant oil providing emollient fatty acids and ginsenoside antioxidants with nourishing and skin-conditioning properties. It is well-tolerated and used in anti-aging and nourishing formulas for its combined emollient and antioxidant content.

Pancratium maritimum (sea daffodil) extract is a Mediterranean coastal plant extract providing lycorine alkaloids and flavonoid antioxidants with brightening and antioxidant properties. It is well-tolerated and used in brightening and protective formulas for its antioxidant and skin-illuminating content.

Panthenol (provitamin B5) is converted to pantothenic acid in the skin, where it functions as a potent humectant, barrier-repairing agent, and anti-inflammatory active that accelerates wound healing and reduces skin sensitivity. It is exceptionally well-tolerated by all skin types including reactive and compromised skin — one of the most reliable and evidence-backed barrier-repair ingredients in modern skincare.

Panthenol (provitamin B5) — listed here with its functional category in parentheses — is converted to pantothenic acid in the skin and functions as a potent humectant, barrier-repairing agent, and anti-inflammatory active. It is exceptionally well-tolerated by all skin types and is one of the most reliable and evidence-backed barrier-repair ingredients in modern skincare.

Pantolactone is a derivative of pantothenic acid (provitamin B5) used in skincare as a skin-conditioning agent with humectant and soothing properties related to the panthenol family. It is considered well-tolerated and non-sensitizing, used in conditioning and barrier-supportive formulas.

Paraffin is a purified petroleum-derived wax used in cosmetics as a thickener, emollient, and occlusive barrier former in lip balms and protective formulas. It is considered inert, non-comedogenic, and well-tolerated — its occlusive properties effectively prevent transepidermal water loss, though it lacks the active skin-repairing properties of plant-derived waxes.

Paraffinum liquidum (mineral oil) is a highly purified petroleum-derived liquid used as an emollient and occlusive moisturizer that prevents transepidermal water loss by forming a protective film on skin. It is non-comedogenic at cosmetic-grade purity, non-sensitizing, and exceptionally well-tolerated — the safety concerns historically associated with mineral oil were based on crude industrial forms, not the highly refined cosmetic grade.

This listing — 'Parfum (F#11116-052)' — is a fragrance formulation listed with an internal batch or formula code, indicating an undisclosed scent blend. Like all parfum or fragrance listings, it may legally contain dozens of sensitizing compounds under a single term without individually naming them; those with fragrance sensitivity should approach with caution.

This parfum blend explicitly discloses three constituent essential oils — lavender (Lavandula angustifolia), Roman chamomile (Anthemis nobilis), and ylang ylang (Cananga odorata) — all of which contain documented fragrance allergens including linalool, linalyl acetate, geraniol, and benzyl benzoate. It is a sensitization risk for reactive or fragrance-sensitive skin, particularly in leave-on products.

Parfum/fragrance is the dual English/French listing of an undisclosed scent blend. A formula can legally contain dozens of sensitizing compounds under this single term — it is one of the leading causes of allergic contact dermatitis in skincare, and fragrance-free products are the safest choice for reactive or sensitive skin.

PEG-10 dimethicone is a PEG-modified silicone copolymer used as an emulsifier and texture modifier in water-in-silicone formulas, enabling stable combination of water and silicone phases while providing a smooth, lightweight skin feel. It is generally well-tolerated, though individuals with PEG sensitivity should note the polyethylene glycol modification.

PEG-10 isostearate is a PEG-modified isostearic acid ester used as a mild emulsifier and solubilizer in skincare formulas. It is generally well-tolerated at typical concentrations, though individuals with PEG sensitivity should note the polyethylene glycol modification.

PEG-100 stearate is a high-ethoxylation PEG-modified stearate widely used as a mild, gentle emulsifier in lotions and creams, helping to create stable oil-in-water emulsions with a smooth texture. Its high ethoxylation number makes it milder and less sensitizing than shorter-chain PEG variants; it is generally well-tolerated across skin types.

PEG-12 dimethicone is a PEG-modified silicone copolymer used as an emulsifier and sensory modifier in water-in-silicone formulas, contributing a smooth, lightweight texture. It is generally well-tolerated, though individuals with PEG sensitivity should note the polyethylene glycol modification.

PEG-120 methyl glucose dioleate is a PEG-modified sugar ester used as a thickener and mild emulsifier in water-based formulas, providing viscosity and stable emulsion formation. It is generally well-tolerated and non-sensitizing, though individuals with PEG sensitivity should note the polyethylene glycol modification.

PEG-150 pentaerythrityl tetrastearate is a high-ethoxylation PEG-modified stearate used as a gentle emulsifier and thickener in skincare formulas. Its high ethoxylation number makes it a mild emulsifier choice; individuals with PEG sensitivity should note the polyethylene glycol modification.

PEG-20 (polyethylene glycol 20) is a synthetic polymer used in skincare as a mild humectant, solubilizer, and co-emulsifier. It is generally well-tolerated at typical cosmetic use concentrations, though individuals with PEG sensitivity should note the polyethylene glycol base.

PEG-20 glyceryl triisostearate is a PEG-modified glyceryl ester used as an emulsifier in anhydrous and oil-phase formulas to help disperse and stabilize formula components. It is generally well-tolerated, though individuals with PEG sensitivity should note the polyethylene glycol modification.

PEG-20 sorbitan isostearate is a PEG-modified sorbitan ester used as a mild emulsifier and solubilizer in skincare formulas, helping to create stable emulsions and disperse oil-soluble ingredients. It is generally well-tolerated, though individuals with PEG sensitivity should note the polyethylene glycol modification.

PEG-200 hydrogenated glyceryl palmate is a high-ethoxylation PEG-modified palm-derived glyceryl ester used as a gentle emulsifier and skin-conditioning agent in cleansers and emulsions. It is generally well-tolerated; individuals with PEG sensitivity should note the polyethylene glycol modification.
